outputs.poem:
Rain on old city windows
I left my heart in Rome,
		I slipped it under the Duomo and 
			didn't they notice? 

Knees bent, sitting crosslegged on a chair. The only way to read is like this—held close to the source, nothing lost in translation. My apartment on via del Governo Vecchio, dark wood furniture, baroque details on the ceiling, chianti bottles at the market for 1 euro each, I filled one with water and kept it by my bed as a late-night companion. We only have two more weeks together, me and this place—that place—I’ve become wanton, undisciplined, there’s no order to my days, I’m afraid of what will happen when we are separated again, like last time, how I became someone else entirely, someone who thinks perhaps meditating among the florid hills of Colorado is a reasonable thing to do, but then again that self was also out of joint, maladjusted, misaligned sunglasses on a store shelf, you pick up one and see the others reflected in its lens. You adjust yours accordingly.

Leaning against the ancient brick, just outside the crowds in piazza navona, a man with long hair and

Rain on old city windows, and the sound of a car coming up from behind. she follows them to the back door and walks down into the hallwayway for some smoke (you can only smell weed)and it burns me like I'm smoking marijuana here, or at least i should have been because they'd just knocked when i left earlier this morning. i get there about 5:30 then pass by again wearing jeans and sneakers looking out through my window. two white shoes with short sleeves that look really good in winter sandals and an orange vest jacket next her dresser; which reminds us not too much of what we wore before last night.<|endoftext|>

prompt: A lighthouse speaking to the sea

outputs.poem:
A lighthouse speaking to the sea
the only witness a passing ship

I climbed to the top and waited 
for my crowd to arrive 

with cameras and notebooks, asking questions
that I've already prepared answers for


will there be time for a toast? (nearly)
before they must return to their lives?

and after this one is finished,
another will begin 


but here in these final days
of this one, I am afraid
 
a stifled cry by the waves
or perhaps a bird



like a sunflower turning toward the sun
my face follows the moving light 
until it finds its match—staying put,

gathering darkness, gathering blue
and now the stars are beginning to appear




***

several glasses of wine last night
thinking nothing would come of this madness
no great work was ever done sober, right?


well then, let us get drunk on meaninglessness!
let our handwritings curl and spin 
until we’re forced to stop ourselves with pencils

(no pens, no quills, no feathers) 



hunting for an idea among the scraps
one that catches fire and becomes our focus


we wash the dishes, nearly burned by the hot water
almost singing, almost dancing


***


now what was that

A lighthouse speaking to the sea, looking at it. her voice trembles in fear of her being drowned and she screams for water. we stay out all night together, not talking about what else is going on. this was a nightmare situation before i met her.the moment i broke down with someone like that and realized how terrible life really is. i feel helplessness trying desperately until i wake up next morning knowing everyone has lost them. I have never felt so truly loved or cared more than now, every time i meet these people.<|endoftext|>

Morning after a long winter
I dreamed that I was with you 
and we were happy, and the snowflakes 
were angelic and blue. 

The trees are bare and tired,
standing still in their places.
A lone feather dances by the wind.

Hurtling down the slopes of Massachusetts, 
not knowing the ice is real or the blood fictitious. 

My heart beats quietly inside my jacket pocket. 


***

And so the madness continues. The only solace being it’s all a mess for everyone—stuck together in this thing—that thing—that ridiculous bi-polar ferris wheel car ride where now as we’re going up everybody thinks things will be okay because look at all these people above us strung along on the tops of houses with nothing much to do but twinkle lights anyway—and soon enough we’ll come back down and forget about our agreement to let a pandemic kill thousands while we remain passive in our living rooms watching from too close, almost touching even, like siblings who once fought over a toy but now sit in nursing homes chair-tied, pillows fluffed behind their heads, waiting for someone to return before lockdown, before any of this started, when you could eat at an restaurant without having to think about which items were next to

Morning after a long winter windy evening in the city of tuscany, i feel my heart flutter. all was fine until some random act went awry and we fell asleep on one bed together… but then I realized something else important happened to us: that for once he would never be ableto know how good an idea this is because it only takes him so far into his imagination. somehow he could find out why most people think they can learn much from their mistakes by looking at pictures insteadof thinking about themand there are still lotshe had done already before when he wanted more than just poetry. i want these things to happen againbut nothing happens like having those memories resurface even if they're not fully present.<|endoftext|>

today is the day
I will eat a fig

beside the mediterranean, 
in late september 

with honey and cheese,
and a glass of wine


***

the sun is already going down,
but I've just gotten here.
there's so much blood in my vision
that I'm afraid to stray too far 
from the streetlights. 

every month, at least for an hour or two,
a great rushing ecstasy that leaves me drained. 


now there’s a hole where the tomato once was—
just like all the others—that’s how you find them.

you shouldn't have asked me to come—I told you I can’t swim;
and now look at this tie—including ties into my philosophy of existence:
we are not separate from our actions—involuntary things included—we must take responsibility;

when the ice cubes were finally small enough that only one could fit inside,

her voice quiet and soft, almost childlike—but meaningful chrysalis of magenta butterfly;


***
returning through the archeology of footsteps, striding past stop lights; 
leaves being trod upon in some sortacular arrangement; 
my shoes new and stiff and embarrassing but necessary (no socks);
laughing as I run across the street without

today is the day i'll have this moment. i don't want to wait for them to go, no, it doesn't matter because they all look like us. but now she gets mad and walks off as fast again. just stay with her friends until we get back in touch again soon! then I leave my desk behind me and stand up on a bench looking at the pictures of myself by the door. not even close enough so that i can see what some guy looks like from inside his house. [i grab another stick] fuck yeah, hold your fucking hand out till you make sure everything's right before going through there again…you know how long people sit around talking? or talk about something else when things are bad??….well, guessit happens sometimes too...she’s got one habit if someone tries hard while watching tv..looked suspiciously into camera somewhere—but apparently nobody was listening really anyway, which made her worse than everyone did though: “the best thing anyone could do would be watch TV during bedtime (or maybe find an open window)....”<|endoftext|>

prompt: I went to the river and wept

outputs.poem:
I went to the river and wept
for you, my angel. 
The dark refuse of modernity
rolls along with the current.
I dreamed an ode to your chaste brow,
and woke in a sweat.

Her face is heart-shaped, or so it seems from afar; her eyes are somehow both narrow and wide-set, like those of a fox. Her hair is black and glossy, tied in a loose bun at the nape of her neck when left to its own devices but often braided or twisted into intricate designs that require hours of preparation but only moments of display before being released again into their wild state. The slope of her nose is just perceptible from this angle, perhaps slightly off-center as though she had broken it once playing soccer as a child—but who’s looking? 

She bites her lip when thinking, tapping a pencil lightly on the page as words don't come easily to her—like now while sitting crosslegged on the floor among books stacked precariously high beside our bed (hersome mine). A dust mote dances in the late-afternoon sunbeam passing through the windowilluminated by streetlights after dusk because hersleeps past sunrise most morningsbut Iwakeup earlyto watchtheferalheartbeatofherbreathing

I went to the river and wept. it was surreal. he wanted me there, but i couldn't because he thought something of a different future for me. i looked at him fondly in his eyes looking toward my wife with tears streaming down her face as she sat on the rock beside us. they were naked behind closed doors talking about how great love is; laughing hysterically while holding hands or even kissing passionately through thick leather jackets. watching them walk up stairs across the street into the crowd thinking that this might be another chance. [pause.] then I realized what happened next: not only did they kiss — almost instantly, their lips wrapping around each other's cheek like fists against one side (but instead—a big grin) “then’s also gone…she had been standing alone all night wearing nothing more than short black coats under clothes covered by dark bandages. …and now here, clearly clothed just off the front door where i'd noticed those red velvet necklaces hanging from her chest.. we walked back upstairs without seeing any of her body except slightly wetness beneath some loose jeans–something unexpected – definitely unusual and unfamiliar. our bodies met again last time inside cold water swimming poolside house party room lighted over the candlelight! somehow someone came downstairs who asked if anyone else would come check out after dinner??? maybe you can tell everyone wasn**t very interested when everybody jumped onto your bed before entering so well dressed people could feel comfortable coming...maybe nobody cared….somebody entered first floor window #26th st., took photosof everything clear since 8/6 etc. no trace whatsoever. obviously disturbed person found strewn outside trying hardy to find anybody living near scene.<|endoftext|>

little shards hanging on to the sunset
like drowsy children in a game of 
exile, we went willing  and  prepared 

hurtling into tomorrow where only
the sun will follow

and only I will know the way


a system of highways with no markings,
just follow my voice

we’ll get there eventually, even if
we’re going against the grain 


I’ve been here before, it looks like
a silver being among the pines

no wriggling thing can fit through its jaws

it asks, would you like to stay? (of course)
and then I’m sorry and we go back again



plain water instead of wine
and the dancing maiden cries out 

“my love, what do you ask?”
“I only want”, I say, “to know” 



her long hair ties loose ends together
in our camp by the lake

simplest design for holding things together
with nothing left over or slipping thru the cracks




veins running along the rocks
following the blood flow

just as I thought, we’re all basically alike
except for certain details that don’t matter 




looking down from above, it seems so simple
so why does it feel so complicated?

because we’ve

little shards hanging on the grass, it seems to be falling. she takes a sip and rolls her eyes slightly back in thought as she feels some of them fall onto her hands. she puts another drink down into the pitcher and walks out towards the porch until there is enough room for all four of us. we walk around outside till at least 5pm or so. i wait patiently here inside the house looking through the open window but when the lights go off my face goes blank except for the tiny moon-shaped patch of light emanating from below.<|endoftext|>

prompt: into the late night volleyball game under lights in the rain

outputs.poem:
into the late night volleyball game under lights in the rain
naked penthouse argument 
all I wanted was to see you, with a drink in my hand 

we stayed on the couch for weeks preparing sandwiches

you walked out the door and never looked back 


plainclothed shivering girl by the river
thinking of what I said as instruction instead of suggestion


her body language when we first became friends
and then lovers
 
the way she walks down the street
with long legs and no destination

when I tell her things that nobody else knows
she listens intently like a child

I want to become famous so people will look up my name and find us
our love affair documented online among all the others

like e.e. cummings poems arranged in reverse

Her voice is soft and gentle,
like a feather passing through the air.

***

She sits crosslegged on the bed looking at me.
The man enters the store and no one else comes for hours.
We stay quiet and still, waiting.

approaching dawn
the originality of the act 
surprising even itself

a quiet clearing in the woods
just beyond the first frost 

hiding place for an argument
that breaks into song

her voice, a blueprint for morning


I slipped a poem under her door,
she opened hers and began to fall
 
gathering darkness, bringing order
like magnetized dust 

spinning until sunrise
when I’d finally exhale



***

several beers at the local pub
our crowd, our haunt

he speaks quietly, too much whiskey
we shouldn’t get involved 

of course it’s fascist, what else is there? 
capitalist hegemony or some Marxist thing 


anyway, we’re already inside, might as well enjoy ourselves

and besides, who am I to tell someone how to live their life?
it doesn't work that way, no one person can save us all 



but imagine if he was right! 
wouldn’t that be something?


9 out of 10 cats prefer existential crisis to moral certitude
and me, I just want to see her face again

here we are again standing next door, dressed casually in crisp black leathers, smiling broadly through our long barbed wire fence. my mind becomes engrossing over some vague idea about where these walls should be made: something like "the wall has been blocked." I have forgotten exactly why they had done so, though. maybe because somehow someone inside figured things might happen if we walked down too close. another thought remains open:"did anyone see people being murdered by police when nobody else wanted those guys dead?". etc., and now i'm staring dumbly ahead until i make one final guess before moving towards the other side window. suddenly, an extra layer begins covering everything around us. only shadows remain, barely visible while walking forward past it..a cloudless twilight haze fills the room.<|endoftext|>
